name: PANCREATIC ACINAR CELL CARCINOMA
definition: rare neoplasm of exocrine origin disseminated subcutaneous + intraosseous fat necrosis (usually distal to knees / elbows)

demographics.Mean age: 62 (range, 40-81) years; M÷F = 86÷14; 87% Caucasian; more common than ductal

discriminating_features: punctate / chunky peripheral / central tumor calcifications (33-50%)
enhancement less than normal pancreas (greater than ductal adenocarcinomas + less than islet

general: punctate / chunky peripheral / central tumor calcifications (33-50%)
enhancement less than normal pancreas (greater than ductal adenocarcinomas + less than islet
moderately vascular tumor + neovascularity + arterial and venous encasement
